verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचतं वीक्ष्य कृष्णानुचरं व्रजस्‍त्रिय:प्रलम्बबाहुं नवकञ्जलोचनम् ।पीताम्बरं पुष्करमालिनं लस-न्मुखारविन्दं परिमृष्टकुण्डलम् ॥ १ ॥सुविस्मिता: कोऽयमपीव्यदर्शन:कुतश्च कस्याच्युतवेषभूषण: ।इति स्म सर्वा: परिवव्रुरुत्सुका-स्तमुत्तम:श्लोकपदाम्बुजाश्रयम् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācataṁ vīkṣya kṛṣānucaraṁ vraja-striyaḥpralamba-bāhuṁ nava-kañja-locanampītāmbaraṁ puṣkara-mālinaṁ lasan-mukhāravindaṁ parimṛṣṭa-kuṇḍalam
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: The young women of Vraja became astonished upon seeing Lord Kṛṣṇa’s servant, who had long arms, whose eyes resembled a newly grown lotus, who wore a yellow garment and a lotus garland, and whose lotuslike face glowed with brightly polished earrings. “Who is this handsome man?” the gopīs asked. “Where has he come from, and whom does he serve? He’s wearing Kṛṣṇa’s clothes and ornaments!” Saying this, the gopīs eagerly crowded around Uddhava, whose shelter was the lotus feet of Lord Uttamaḥśloka, Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: तं प्रश्रयेणावनता: सुसत्कृतंसव्रीडहासेक्षणसूनृतादिभि: ।रहस्यपृच्छन्नुपविष्टमासनेविज्ञाय सन्देशहरं रमापते: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ praśrayeṇāvanatāḥ su-sat-kṛtaṁsa-vrīḍa-hāsekṣaṇa-sūnṛtādibhiḥrahasy apṛcchann upaviṣṭam āsanevijñāya sandeśa-haraṁ ramā-pateḥ
verses.text: Bowing their heads in humility, the gopīs duly honored Uddhava with their shy, smiling glances and pleasing words. They took him to a quiet place, seated him comfortably and began to question him, for they recognized him to be a messenger from Kṛṣṇa, the master of the goddess of fortune.

verses.sanskrit: जानीमस्त्वां यदुपते: पार्षदं समुपागतम् ।भर्त्रेह प्रेषित: पित्रोर्भवान् प्रियचिकीर्षया ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: jānīmas tvāṁ yadu-pateḥpārṣadaṁ samupāgatambhartreha preṣitaḥ pitrorbhavān priya-cikīrṣayā
verses.text: [The gopīs said:] We know that you are the personal servant of Kṛṣṇa, the chief of the Yadus, and that you have come here on the order of your good master, who desires to give pleasure to His parents.

verses.sanskrit: अन्यथा गोव्रजे तस्य स्मरणीयं न चक्ष्महे ।स्‍नेहानुबन्धो बन्धूनां मुनेरपि सुदुस्त्यज: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: anyathā go-vraje tasyasmaraṇīyaṁ na cakṣmahesnehānubandho bandhūnāṁmuner api su-dustyajaḥ
verses.text: We see nothing else He might consider worth remembering in these cow pastures of Vraja. Indeed, the bonds of affection for one’s family members are difficult to break, even for a sage.

verses.sanskrit: अन्येष्वर्थकृता मैत्री यावदर्थविडम्बनम् ।पुम्भि: स्‍त्रीषु कृता यद्वत् सुमन:स्विव षट्पदै: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: anyeṣv artha-kṛtā maitrīyāvad-artha-viḍambanampumbhiḥ strīṣu kṛtā yadvatsumanaḥsv iva ṣaṭpadaiḥ
verses.text: The friendship shown toward others — those who are not family members — is motivated by personal interest, and thus it is a pretense that lasts only until one’s purpose is fulfilled. Such friendship is just like the interest men take in women, or bees in flowers.

verses.sanskrit: नि:स्वं त्यजन्ति गणिका अकल्पं नृपतिं प्रजा: ।अधीतविद्या आचार्यमृत्विजो दत्तदक्षिणम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: niḥsvaṁ tyajanti gaṇikāakalpaṁ nṛpatiṁ prajāḥadhīta-vidyā ācāryamṛtvijo datta-dakṣiṇam
verses.text: Prostitutes abandon a penniless man, subjects an incompetent king, students their teacher once they have finished their education, and priests a man who has remunerated them for a sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: खगा वीतफलं वृक्षं भुक्त्वा चातिथयो गृहम् ।दग्धं मृगास्तथारण्यं जारा भुक्त्वा रतां स्‍त्रियम् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: khagā vīta-phalaṁ vṛkṣaṁbhuktvā cātithayo gṛhamdagdhaṁ mṛgās tathāraṇyaṁjārā bhuktvā ratāṁ striyam
verses.text: Birds abandon a tree when its fruits are gone, guests a house after they have eaten, animals a forest that has burnt down, and a lover the woman he has enjoyed, even though she remains attached to him.

verses.sanskrit: इति गोप्यो हि गोविन्दे गतवाक्‍कायमानसा: ।कृष्णदूते समायाते उद्धवे त्यक्तलौकिका: ॥ ९ ॥गायन्त्य: प्रियकर्माणि रुदन्त्यश्च गतह्रिय: ।तस्य संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य यानि कैशोरबाल्ययो: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: iti gopyo hi govindegata-vāk-kāya-mānasāḥkṛṣṇa-dūte samāyāteuddhave tyakta-laukikāḥ
verses.text: Thus speaking, the gopīs, whose words, bodies and minds were fully dedicated to Lord Govinda, put aside all their regular work now that Kṛṣṇa’s messenger, Śrī Uddhava, had arrived among them. Constantly remembering the activities their beloved Kṛṣṇa had performed in His childhood and youth, they sang about them and cried without shame.

verses.sanskrit: काचिन्मधुकरं द‍ृष्ट्वा ध्यायन्ती कृष्णसङ्गमम् ।प्रियप्रस्थापितं दूतं कल्पयित्वेदमब्रवीत् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: kācin madhukaraṁ dṛṣṭvādhyāyantī kṛṣṇa-saṅgamampriya-prasthāpitaṁ dūtaṁkalpayitvedam abravīt
verses.text: One of the gopīs, while meditating on Her previous association with Kṛṣṇa, saw a honeybee before Her and imagined it to be a messenger sent by Her beloved. Thus She spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: गोप्युवाचमधुप कितवबन्धो मा स्पृशाङ्‍‍घ्रिं सपत्न्‍या:कुचविलुलितमालाकुङ्कुमश्मश्रुभिर्न: ।वहतु मधुपतिस्तन्मानिनीनां प्रसादंयदुसदसि विडम्ब्यं यस्य दूतस्त्वमीद‍ृक् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: gopy uvācamadhupa kitava-bandho mā spṛśaṅghriṁ sapatnyāḥkuca-vilulita-mālā-kuṅkuma-śmaśrubhir naḥvahatu madhu-patis tan-māninīnāṁ prasādaṁyadu-sadasi viḍambyaṁ yasya dūtas tvam īdṛk
verses.text: The gopī said: O honeybee, O friend of a cheater, don’t touch My feet with your whiskers, which are smeared with the kuṅkuma that rubbed onto Kṛṣṇa’s garland when it was crushed by the breasts of a rival lover! Let Kṛṣṇa satisfy the women of Mathurā. One who sends a messenger like you will certainly be ridiculed in the Yadus’ assembly.

verses.sanskrit: सकृदधरसुधां स्वां मोहिनीं पाययित्वासुमनस इव सद्यस्तत्यजेऽस्मान् भवाद‍ृक् ।परिचरति कथं तत्पादपद्मं नु पद्माह्यपि बत हृतचेता ह्युत्तम:श्लोकजल्पै: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sakṛd adhara-sudhāṁ svāṁ mohinīṁ pāyayitvāsumanasa iva sadyas tatyaje ’smān bhavādṛkparicarati kathaṁ tat-pāda-padmaṁ nu padmāhy api bata hṛta-cetā hy uttamaḥ-śloka-jalpaiḥ
verses.text: After making us drink the enchanting nectar of His lips only once, Kṛṣṇa suddenly abandoned us, just as you might quickly abandon some flowers. How is it, then, that Goddess Padmā willingly serves His lotus feet? Alas! The answer must certainly be that her mind has been stolen away by His deceitful words.

verses.sanskrit: किमिह बहु षडङ्‍‍घ्रे गायसि त्वं यदूना-मधिपतिमगृहाणामग्रतो न: पुराणम् ।विजयसखसखीनां गीयतां तत्प्रसङ्ग:क्षपितकुचरुजस्ते कल्पयन्तीष्टमिष्टा: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kim iha bahu ṣaḍ-aṅghre gāyasi tvaṁ yadūnāmadhipatim agṛhāṇām agrato naḥ purāṇamvijaya-sakha-sakhīnāṁ gīyatāṁ tat-prasaṅgaḥkṣapita-kuca-rujas te kalpayantīṣṭam iṣṭāḥ
verses.text: O bee, why do you sing here so much about the Lord of the Yadus, in front of us homeless people? These topics are old news to us. Better you sing about that friend of Arjuna in front of His new girlfriends, the burning desire in whose breasts He has now relieved. Those ladies will surely give you the charity you are begging.

verses.sanskrit: दिवि भुवि च रसायां का: स्‍त्रियस्तद्दुरापा:कपटरुचिरहासभ्रूविजृम्भस्य या: स्यु: ।चरणरज उपास्ते यस्य भूतिर्वयं काअपि च कृपणपक्षे ह्युत्तम:श्लोकशब्द: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: divi bhuvi ca rasāyāṁ kāḥ striyas tad-durāpāḥkapaṭa-rucira-hāsa-bhrū-vijṛmbhasya yāḥ syuḥcaraṇa-raja upāste yasya bhūtir vayaṁ kāapi ca kṛpaṇa-pakṣe hy uttamaḥ-śloka-śabdaḥ
verses.text: In heaven, on earth or in the subterranean sphere, what women are unavailable to Him? He simply arches His eyebrows and smiles with deceptive charm, and they all become His. The supreme goddess herself worships the dust of His feet, so what is our position in comparison? But at least those who are wretched can chant His name, Uttamaḥśloka.

verses.sanskrit: विसृज शिरसि पादं वेद्‍म्यहं चाटुकारै-रनुनयविदुषस्तेऽभ्येत्य दौत्यैर्मुकुन्दात् ।स्वकृत इह विसृष्टापत्यपत्यन्यलोकाव्यसृजदकृतचेता: किं नु सन्धेयमस्मिन् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: visṛja śirasi pādaṁ vedmy ahaṁ cātu-kārairanunaya-viduṣas te ’bhyetya dautyair mukundātsva-kṛta iha viṣṛṣṭāpatya-paty-anya-lokāvyasṛjad akṛta-cetāḥ kiṁ nu sandheyam asmin
verses.text: Keep your head off My feet! I know what you’re doing. You expertly learned diplomacy from Mukunda, and now you come as His messenger with flattering words. But He abandoned those who for His sake alone gave up their children, husbands and all other relations. He’s simply ungrateful. Why should I make up with Him now?

verses.sanskrit: मृगयुरिव कपीन्द्रं विव्यधे लुब्धधर्मास्‍त्रियमकृत विरूपां स्‍त्रीजित: कामयानाम् ।बलिमपि बलिमत्त्वावेष्टयद् ध्वाङ्‍क्षवद्-यस्तदलमसितसख्यैर्दुस्त्यजस्तत्कथार्थ: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: mṛgayur iva kapīndraṁ vivyadhe lubdha-dharmāstriyam akṛta virūpāṁ strī-jitaḥ kāma-yānāmbalim api balim attvāveṣṭayad dhvāṅkṣa-vad yastad alam asita-sakhyair dustyajas tat-kathārthaḥ
verses.text: Like a hunter, He cruelly shot the king of the monkeys with arrows. Because He was conquered by a woman, He disfigured another woman who came to Him with lusty desires. And even after consuming the gifts of Bali Mahārāja, He bound him up with ropes as if he were a crow. So let us give up all friendship with this dark-complexioned boy, even if we can’t give up talking about Him.

verses.sanskrit: यदनुचरितलीलाकर्णपीयूषविप्रुट्-सकृददनविधूतद्वन्द्वधर्मा विनष्टा: ।सपदि गृहकुटुम्बं दीनमुत्सृज्य दीनाबहव इह विहङ्गा भिक्षुचर्यां चरन्ति ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: yad-anucarita-līlā-karṇa-pīyūṣa-vipruṭ-sakṛd-adana-vidhūta-dvandva-dharmā vinaṣṭāḥsapadi gṛha-kuṭumbaṁ dīnam utsṛjya dīnābahava iha vihaṅgā bhikṣu-caryāṁ caranti
verses.text: To hear about the pastimes that Kṛṣṇa regularly performs is nectar for the ears. For those who relish just a single drop of that nectar, even once, their dedication to material duality is ruined. Many such persons have suddenly given up their wretched homes and families and, themselves becoming wretched, traveled here to Vṛndāvana to wander about like birds, begging for their living.

verses.sanskrit: वयमृतमिव जिह्मव्याहृतं श्रद्दधाना:कुलिकरुतमिवाज्ञा: कृष्णवध्वो हरिण्य: ।दद‍ृशुरसकृदेतत्तन्नखस्पर्शतीव्र-स्मररुज उपमन्त्रिन् भण्यतामन्यवार्ता ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vayam ṛtam iva jihma-vyāhṛtaṁ śraddadhānāḥkulika-rutam ivājñāḥ kṛṣṇa-vadhvo hariṇyaḥdadṛśur asakṛd etat tan-nakha-sparśa-tīvrasmara-ruja upamantrin bhaṇyatām anya-vārtā
verses.text: Faithfully taking His deceitful words as true, we became just like the black deer’s foolish wives, who trust the cruel hunter’s song. Thus we repeatedly felt the sharp pain of lust caused by the touch of His nails. O messenger, please talk about something besides Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: प्रियसख पुनरागा: प्रेयसा प्रेषित: किंवरय किमनुरुन्धे माननीयोऽसि मेऽङ्ग ।नयसि कथमिहास्मान् दुस्त्यजद्वन्द्वपार्श्वंसततमुरसि सौम्य श्रीर्वधू: साकमास्ते ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: priya-sakha punar āgāḥ preyasā preṣitaḥ kiṁvaraya kim anurundhe mānanīyo ’si me ’ṅganayasi katham ihāsmān dustyaja-dvandva-pārśvaṁsatatam urasi saumya śrīr vadhūḥ sākam āste
verses.text: O friend of My dear one, has My beloved sent you here again? I should honor you, friend, so please choose whatever boon you wish. But why have you come back here to take us to Him, whose conjugal love is so difficult to give up? After all, gentle bee, His consort is the goddess Śrī, and she is always with Him, staying upon His chest.

verses.sanskrit: अपि बत मधुपुर्यामार्यपुत्रोऽधुनास्तेस्मरति स पितृगेहान् सौम्य बन्धूंश्च गोपान् ।क्व‍‍चिदपि स कथा न: किङ्करीणां गृणीतेभुजमगुरुसुगन्धं मूर्ध्‍न्यधास्यत् कदा नु ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: api bata madhu-puryām ārya-putro ’dhunāstesmarati sa pitṛ-gehān saumya bandhūṁś ca gopānkvacid api sa kathā naḥ kiṅkarīṇāṁ gṛṇītebhujam aguru-sugandhaṁ mūrdhny adhāsyat kadā nu
verses.text: O Uddhava! It is indeed regrettable that Kṛṣṇa resides in Mathurā. Does He remember His father’s household affairs and His friends, the cowherd boys? O great soul! Does He ever talk about us, His maidservants? When will He lay on our heads His aguru-scented hand?

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचअथोद्धवो निशम्यैवं कृष्णदर्शनलालसा: ।सान्‍त्‍वयन् प्रियसन्देशैर्गोपीरिदमभाषत ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaathoddhavo niśamyaivaṁkṛṣṇa-darśana-lālasāḥsāntvayan priya-sandeśairgopīr idam abhāṣata
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Having heard this, Uddhava then tried to pacify the gopīs, who were most eager to see Lord Kṛṣṇa. He thus began relating to them the message of their beloved.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीउद्धव उवाचअहो यूयं स्म पूर्णार्था भवत्यो लोकपूजिता: ।वासुदेवे भगवति यासामित्यर्पितं मन: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvācaaho yūyaṁ sma pūrṇārthābhavatyo loka-pūjitāḥvāsudeve bhagavatiyāsām ity arpitaṁ manaḥ
verses.text: Śrī Uddhava said: Certainly you gopīs are all-successful and are universally worshiped because you have dedicated your minds in this way to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Vāsudeva.

verses.sanskrit: दानव्रततपोहोम जपस्वाध्यायसंयमै: ।श्रेयोभिर्विविधैश्चान्यै: कृष्णे भक्तिर्हि साध्यते ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: dāna-vrata-tapo-homajapa-svādhyāya-saṁyamaiḥśreyobhir vividhaiś cānyaiḥkṛṣṇe bhaktir hi sādhyate
verses.text: Devotional service unto Lord Kṛṣṇa is attained by charity, strict vows, austerities and fire sacrifices, by japa, study of Vedic texts, observance of regulative principles and, indeed, by the performance of many other auspicious practices.

verses.sanskrit: भगवत्युत्तम:श्लोके भवतीभिरनुत्तमा ।भक्ति: प्रवर्तिता दिष्‍ट्या मुनीनामपि दुर्लभा ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavaty uttamaḥ-ślokebhavatībhir anuttamābhaktiḥ pravartitā diṣṭyāmunīnām api durlabhā
verses.text: By your great fortune you have established an unexcelled standard of pure devotion for the Lord, Uttamaḥśloka — a standard even the sages can hardly attain.

verses.sanskrit: दिष्‍ट्या पुत्रान्पतीन्देहान् स्वजनान्भवनानि च ।हित्वावृणीत यूयं यत् कृष्णाख्यं पुरुषं परम् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: diṣṭyā putrān patīn dehānsva-janān bhavanāni cahitvāvṛnīta yūyaṁ yatkṛṣṇākhyaṁ puruṣaṁ param
verses.text: By your great fortune you have left your sons, husbands, bodily comforts, relatives and homes in favor of the supreme male, who is known as Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वात्मभावोऽधिकृतो भवतीनामधोक्षजे ।विरहेण महाभागा महान्मेऽनुग्रह: कृत: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarvātma-bhāvo ’dhikṛtobhavatīnām adhokṣajeviraheṇa mahā-bhāgāmahān me ’nugrahaḥ kṛtaḥ
verses.text: You have rightfully claimed the privilege of unalloyed love for the transcendental Lord, O most glorious gopīs. Indeed, by exhibiting your love for Kṛṣṇa in separation from Him, you have shown me great mercy.

verses.sanskrit: श्रूयतां प्रियसन्देशो भवतीनां सुखावह: ।यमादायागतो भद्रा अहं भर्तू रहस्कर: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrūyatāṁ priya-sandeśobhavatīnāṁ sukhāvahaḥyam ādāyāgato bhadrāahaṁ bhartū rahas-karaḥ
verses.text: My good ladies, now please hear your beloved’s message, which I, the confidential servant of my master, have come here to bring you.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचभवतीनां वियोगो मे न हि सर्वात्मना क्व‍‍चित् ।यथा भूतानि भूतेषु खं वाय्वग्निर्जलं मही ।तथाहं च मन:प्राणभूतेन्द्रियगुणाश्रय: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācabhavatīnāṁ viyogo mena hi sarvātmanā kvacityathā bhūtāni bhūteṣukhaṁ vāyv-agnir jalaṁ mahītathāhaṁ ca manaḥ-prāṇa-bhūtendriya-guṇāśrayaḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Lord said: You are never actually separated from Me, for I am the Soul of all creation. Just as the elements of nature — ether, air, fire, water and earth — are present in every created thing, so I am present within everyone’s mind, life air and senses, and also within the physical elements and the modes of material nature.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मन्येवात्मनात्मानं सृजे हन्म्यनुपालये ।आत्ममायानुभावेन भूतेन्द्रियगुणात्मना ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: ātmany evātmanātmānaṁsṛje hanmy anupālayeātma-māyānubhāvenabhūtendriya-guṇātmanā
verses.text: By Myself I create, sustain and withdraw Myself within Myself by the power of My personal energy, which comprises the material elements, the senses and the modes of nature.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मा ज्ञानमय: शुद्धो व्यतिरिक्तोऽगुणान्वय: ।सुषुप्तिस्वप्नजाग्रद्भ‍िर्मायावृत्तिभिरीयते ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ātmā jñāna-mayaḥ śuddhovyatirikto ’guṇānvayaḥsuṣupti-svapna-jāgradbhirmāyā-vṛttibhir īyate
verses.text: Being composed of pure consciousness, or knowledge, the soul is distinct from everything material and is uninvolved in the entanglements of the modes of nature. We can perceive the soul through the three functions of material nature known as wakefulness, sleep and deep sleep.

verses.sanskrit: येनेन्द्रियार्थान् ध्यायेत मृषा स्वप्नवदुत्थित: ।तन्निरुन्ध्यादिन्द्रियाणि विनिद्र: प्रत्यपद्यत ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yenendriyārthān dhyāyetamṛṣā svapna-vad utthitaḥtan nirundhyād indriyāṇivinidraḥ pratyapadyata
verses.text: As a person just arisen from sleep may continue to meditate on a dream even though it is illusory, so by the agency of the mind one meditates on the sense objects, which the senses can then obtain. Therefore one should become fully alert and bring the mind under control.

verses.sanskrit: एतदन्त: समाम्नायो योग: साङ्ख्यं मनीषिणाम् ।त्यागस्तपो दम: सत्यं समुद्रान्ता इवापगा: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: etad-antaḥ samāmnāyoyogaḥ sāṅkhyaṁ manīṣiṇāmtyāgas tapo damaḥ satyaṁsamudrāntā ivāpagāḥ
verses.text: According to intelligent authorities, this is the ultimate conclusion of all the Vedas, as well as all practice of yoga, Sāṅkhya, renunciation, austerity, sense control and truthfulness, just as the sea is the ultimate destination of all rivers.

verses.sanskrit: यत्त्वहं भवतीनां वै दूरे वर्ते प्रियो द‍ृशाम् ।मनस: सन्निकर्षार्थं मदनुध्यानकाम्यया ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yat tv ahaṁ bhavatīnāṁ vaidūre varte priyo dṛśāmmanasaḥ sannikarṣārthaṁmad-anudhyāna-kāmyayā
verses.text: But the actual reason why I, the beloved object of your sight, have stayed far away from you is that I wanted to intensify your meditation upon Me and thus draw your minds closer to Me.

verses.sanskrit: यथा दूरचरे प्रेष्ठे मन आविश्य वर्तते ।स्‍त्रीणां च न तथा चेत: सन्निकृष्टेऽक्षिगोचरे ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā dūra-care preṣṭhemana āviśya vartatestrīṇāṁ ca na tathā cetaḥsannikṛṣṭe ’kṣi-gocare
verses.text: When her lover is far away, a woman thinks of him more than when he is present before her.

verses.sanskrit: मय्यावेश्य मन: कृत्‍स्‍नं विमुक्ताशेषवृत्ति यत् ।अनुस्मरन्त्यो मां नित्यमचिरान्मामुपैष्यथ ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: mayy āveśya manaḥ kṛtsnaṁvimuktāśeṣa-vṛtti yatanusmarantyo māṁ nityamacirān mām upaiṣyatha
verses.text: Because your minds are totally absorbed in Me and free from all other engagement, you remember Me always, and so you will very soon have Me again in your presence.

verses.sanskrit: या मया क्रीडता रात्र्यां वनेऽस्मिन्व्रज आस्थिता: ।अलब्धरासा: कल्याण्यो मापुर्मद्वीर्यचिन्तया ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: yā mayā krīḍatā rātryāṁvane ’smin vraja āsthitāḥalabdha-rāsāḥ kalyāṇyomāpur mad-vīrya-cintayā
verses.text: Although some gopīs had to remain in the cowherd village and so could not join the rāsa dance to sport with Me at night in the forest, they were nonetheless fortunate. Indeed, they attained Me by thinking of My potent pastimes.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं प्रियतमादिष्टमाकर्ण्य व्रजयोषित: ।ता ऊचुरुद्धवं प्रीतास्तत्सन्देशागतस्मृती: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaevaṁ priyatamādiṣṭamākarṇya vraja-yoṣitaḥtā ūcur uddhavaṁ prītāstat-sandeśāgata-smṛtīḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: The women of Vraja were pleased to hear this message from their dearmost Kṛṣṇa. His words having revived their memory, they addressed Uddhava as follows.

verses.sanskrit: गोप्य ऊचु:दिष्‍ट्याहितो हत: कंसो यदूनां सानुगोऽघकृत् ।दिष्‍ट्याप्तैर्लब्धसर्वार्थै: कुशल्यास्तेऽच्युतोऽधुना ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: gopya ūcuḥdiṣṭyāhito hataḥ kaṁsoyadūnāṁ sānugo ’gha-kṛtdiṣṭyāptair labdha-sarvārthaiḥkuśaly āste ’cyuto ’dhunā
verses.text: The gopīs said: It is very good that Kaṁsa, the enemy and persecutor of the Yadus, has now been killed, along with his followers. And it is also very good that Lord Acyuta is living happily in the company of His well-wishing friends and relatives, whose every desire is now fulfilled.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चिद् गदाग्रज: सौम्य करोति पुरयोषिताम् ।प्रीतिं न: स्‍निग्धसव्रीडहासोदारेक्षणार्चित: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: kaccid gadāgrajaḥ saumyakaroti pura-yoṣitāmprītiṁ naḥ snigdha-savrīḍa-hāsodārekṣaṇārcitaḥ
verses.text: Gentle Uddhava, is the elder brother of Gada now bestowing on the city women the pleasure that actually belongs to us? We suppose those ladies worship Him with generous glances full of affectionate, shy smiles.

verses.sanskrit: कथं रतिविशेषज्ञ: प्रियश्च पुरयोषिताम् ।नानुबध्येत तद्वाक्यैर्विभ्रमैश्चानुभाजित: ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: kathaṁ rati-viśeṣa-jñaḥpriyaś ca pura-yoṣitāmnānubadhyeta tad-vākyairvibhramaiś cānubhājitaḥ
verses.text: Śrī Kṛṣṇa is expert in all kinds of conjugal affairs and is the darling of the city women. How can He not become entangled, now that He’s constantly adored by their enchanting words and gestures?

verses.sanskrit: अपि स्मरति न: साधो गोविन्द: प्रस्तुते क्व‍‍चित् ।गोष्ठिमध्ये पुरस्‍त्रीणां ग्राम्या: स्वैरकथान्तरे ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: api smarati naḥ sādhogovindaḥ prastute kvacitgoṣṭhi-madhye pura-strīṇāmgrāmyāḥ svaira-kathāntare
verses.text: O saintly one, does Govinda ever remember us during His conversations with the city women? Does He ever mention us village girls as He freely talks with them?

verses.sanskrit: ता: किं निशा: स्मरति यासु तदा प्रियाभि-र्वृन्दावने कुमुदकुन्दशशाङ्करम्ये ।रेमे क्व‍णच्चरणनूपुररासगोष्ठ्या-मस्माभिरीडितमनोज्ञकथ: कदाचित् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāḥ kiṁ niśāḥ smarati yāsu tadā priyābhirvṛndāvane kumuda-kunda-śaśāṅka-ramyereme kvaṇac-caraṇa-nūpura-rāsa-goṣṭhyāmasmābhir īḍita-manojña-kathaḥ kadācit
verses.text: Does He recall those nights in the Vṛndāvana forest, lovely with lotus, jasmine and the bright moon? As we glorified His charming pastimes, He enjoyed with us, His beloved girlfriends, in the circle of the rāsa dance, which resounded with the music of ankle bells.

verses.sanskrit: अप्येष्यतीह दाशार्हस्तप्ता: स्वकृतया शुचा ।सञ्जीवयन् नु नो गात्रैर्यथेन्द्रो वनमम्बुदै: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: apy eṣyatīha dāśārhastaptāḥ sva-kṛtayā śucāsañjīvayan nu no gātrairyathendro vanam ambudaiḥ
verses.text: Will that descendant of Daśārha return here and by the touch of His limbs bring back to life those who are now burning with the grief He Himself has caused? Will He save us in that way, just as Lord Indra brings a forest back to life with his water-bearing clouds?

verses.sanskrit: कस्मात् कृष्ण इहायाति प्राप्तराज्यो हताहित: ।नरेन्द्रकन्या उद्वाह्य प्रीत: सर्वसुहृद् वृत: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kasmāt kṛṣṇa ihāyātiprāpta-rājyo hatāhitaḥnarendra-kanyā udvāhyaprītaḥ sarva-suhṛd-vṛtaḥ
verses.text: But why should Kṛṣṇa come here after winning a kingdom, killing His enemies and marrying the daughters of kings? He’s satisfied there, surrounded by all His friends and well-wishers.

verses.sanskrit: किमस्माभिर्वनौकोभिरन्याभिर्वा महात्मन: ।श्रीपतेराप्तकामस्य क्रियेतार्थ: कृतात्मन: ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kim asmābhir vanaukobhiranyābhir vā mahātmanaḥśrī-pater āpta-kāmasyakriyetārthaḥ kṛtātmanaḥ
verses.text: The great soul Kṛṣṇa is the Lord of the goddess of fortune, and He automatically achieves whatever He desires. How can we forest-dwellers or any other women fulfill His purposes when He is already fulfilled within Himself?

verses.sanskrit: परं सौख्यं हि नैराश्यं स्वैरिण्यप्याहपिङ्गला ।तज्जानतीनां न: कृष्णे तथाप्याशा दुरत्यया ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: paraṁ saukhyaṁ hi nairāśyaṁsvairiṇy apy āha piṅgalātaj jānatīnāṁ naḥ kṛṣṇetathāpy āśā duratyayā
verses.text: Indeed, the greatest happiness is to renounce all desires, as even the prostitute Piṅgalā has declared. Yet even though we know this, we cannot give up our hopes of attaining Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: क उत्सहेत सन्त्यक्तुमुत्तम:श्लोकसंविदम् ।अनिच्छतोऽपि यस्य श्रीरङ्गान्न च्यवते क्व‍‍चित् ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: ka utsaheta santyaktumuttamaḥśloka-saṁvidamanicchato ’pi yasya śrīraṅgān na cyavate kvacit
verses.text: Who can bear to give up intimate talks with Lord Uttamaḥśloka? Although He shows no interest in her, Goddess Śrī never moves from her place on His chest.

verses.sanskrit: सरिच्छैलवनोद्देशा गावो वेणुरवा इमे ।सङ्कर्षणसहायेन कृष्णेनाचरिता: प्रभो ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: saric-chaila-vanoddeśāgāvo veṇu-ravā imesaṅkarṣaṇa-sahāyenakṛṣṇenācaritāḥ prabho
verses.text: Dear Uddhava Prabhu, when Kṛṣṇa was here in the company of Saṅkarṣaṇa, He enjoyed all these rivers, hills, forests, cows and flute sounds.

verses.sanskrit: पुन: पुन: स्मारयन्ति नन्दगोपसुतं बत ।श्रीनिकेतैस्तत्पदकैर्विस्मर्तुं नैव शक्नुम: ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: punaḥ punaḥ smārayantinanda-gopa-sutaṁ bataśrī-niketais tat-padakairvismartuṁ naiva śaknumaḥ
verses.text: All these remind us constantly of Nanda’s son. Indeed, because we see Kṛṣṇa’s footprints, which are marked with divine symbols, we can never forget Him.

verses.sanskrit: गत्या ललितयोदारहासलीलावलोकनै: ।माध्व्या गिरा हृतधिय: कथं तं विस्मरामहे ॥ ५१ ॥
verses.transliteration: gatyā lalitayodāra-hāsa-līlāvalokanaiḥmādhvyā girā hṛta-dhiyaḥkathaṁ taṁ vismarāma he
verses.text: O Uddhava, how can we forget Him when our hearts have been stolen away by the charming way He walks, His generous smile and playful glances, and His honeylike words?

verses.sanskrit: हे नाथ हे रमानाथ व्रजनाथार्तिनाशन ।मग्नमुद्धर गोविन्द गोकुलं वृजिनार्णवात् ॥ ५२ ॥
verses.transliteration: he nātha he ramā-nāthavraja-nāthārti-nāśanamagnam uddhara govindagokulaṁ vṛjinārṇavāt
verses.text: O master, O master of the goddess of fortune, O master of Vraja! O destroyer of all suffering, Govinda, please lift Your Gokula out of the ocean of distress in which it is drowning!

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचततस्ता: कृष्णसन्देशैर्व्यपेतविरहज्वरा: ।उद्धवं पूजयां चक्रुर्ज्ञात्वात्मानमधोक्षजम् ॥ ५३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācatatas tāḥ kṛṣṇa-sandeśairvyapeta-viraha-jvarāḥuddhavaṁ pūjayāṁ cakrurjñātvātmānam adhokṣajam
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: Lord Kṛṣṇa’s messages having relieved their fever of separation, the gopīs then worshiped Uddhava, recognizing him as nondifferent from their Lord, Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: उवास कतिचिन्मासान्गोपीनां विनुदन् शुच: ।कृष्णलीलाकथां गायन् रमयामास गोकुलम् ॥ ५४ ॥
verses.transliteration: uvāsa katicin māsāngopīnāṁ vinudan śucaḥkṛṣṇa-līlā-kathāṁ gāyanramayām āsa gokulam
verses.text: Uddhava remained there for several months, dispelling the gopīs’ sorrow by chanting the topics of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s pastimes. Thus he brought joy to all the people of Gokula.

verses.sanskrit: यावन्त्यहानि नन्दस्य व्रजेऽवात्सीत् स उद्धव: ।व्रजौकसां क्षणप्रायाण्यासन् कृष्णस्य वार्तया ॥ ५५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yāvanty ahāni nandasyavraje ’vātsīt sa uddhavaḥvrajaukasāṁ kṣaṇa-prāyāṇyāsan kṛṣṇasya vārtayā
verses.text: All the days that Uddhava dwelled in Nanda’s cowherd village seemed like a single moment to the residents of Vraja, for Uddhava was always discussing Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: सरिद्वनगिरिद्रोणीर्वीक्षन् कुसुमितान् द्रुमान् ।कृष्णं संस्मारयन् रेमे हरिदासो व्रजौकसाम् ॥ ५६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarid-vana-giri-droṇīrvīkṣan kusumitān drumānkṛṣṇaṁ saṁsmārayan remehari-dāso vrajaukasām
verses.text: That servant of Lord Hari, seeing the rivers, forests, mountains, valleys and flowering trees of Vraja, enjoyed inspiring the inhabitants of Vṛndāvana by reminding them of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्ट्वैवमादि गोपीनां कृष्णावेशात्मविक्लवम् ।उद्धव: परमप्रीतस्ता नमस्यन्निदं जगौ ॥ ५७ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvaivam-ādi gopīnāṁkṛṣṇāveśātma-viklavamuddhavaḥ parama-prītastā namasyann idaṁ jagau
verses.text: Thus seeing how the gopīs were always disturbed because of their total absorption in Kṛṣṇa, Uddhava was supremely pleased. Desiring to offer them all respect, he sang as follows.

verses.sanskrit: एता: परं तनुभृतो भुवि गोपवध्वोगोविन्द एव निखिलात्मनि रूढभावा: ।वाञ्छन्ति यद् भवभियो मुनयो वयं चकिं ब्रह्मजन्मभिरनन्तकथारसस्य ॥ ५८ ॥
verses.transliteration: etāḥ paraṁ tanu-bhṛto bhuvi gopa-vadhvogovinda eva nikhilātmani rūḍha-bhāvāḥvāñchanti yad bhava-bhiyo munayo vayaṁ cakiṁ brahma-janmabhir ananta-kathā-rasasya
verses.text: [Uddhava sang:] Among all persons on earth, these cowherd women alone have actually perfected their embodied lives, for they have achieved the perfection of unalloyed love for Lord Govinda. Their pure love is hankered after by those who fear material existence, by great sages, and by ourselves as well. For one who has tasted the narrations of the infinite Lord, what is the use of taking birth as a high-class brāhmaṇa, or even as Lord Brahmā himself?

verses.sanskrit: क्व‍ेमा: स्‍त्रियो वनचरीर्व्यभिचारदुष्टा:कृष्णे क्व‍ चैष परमात्मनि रूढभाव: ।नन्वीश्वरोऽनुभजतोऽविदुषोऽपि साक्षा-च्छ्रेयस्तनोत्यगदराज इवोपयुक्त: ॥ ५९ ॥
verses.transliteration: kvemāḥ striyo vana-carīr vyabhicāra-duṣṭāḥkṛṣṇe kva caiṣa paramātmani rūḍha-bhāvaḥnanv īśvaro ’nubhajato ’viduṣo ’pi sākṣācchreyas tanoty agada-rāja ivopayuktaḥ
verses.text: How amazing it is that these simple women who wander about the forest, seemingly spoiled by improper behavior, have achieved the perfection of unalloyed love for Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Soul! Still, it is true that the Supreme Lord Himself awards His blessings even to an ignorant worshiper, just as the best medicine works even when taken by a person ignorant of its ingredients.

verses.sanskrit: नायं श्रियोऽङ्ग उ नितान्तरते: प्रसाद:स्वर्योषितां नलिनगन्धरुचां कुतोऽन्या: ।रासोत्सवेऽस्य भुजदण्डगृहीतकण्ठ-लब्धाशिषां य उदगाद् व्रजवल्ल‍भीनाम् ॥ ६० ॥
verses.transliteration: nāyaṁ śriyo ’ṅga u nitānta-rateḥ prasādaḥsvar-yoṣitāṁ nalina-gandha-rucāṁ kuto ’nyāḥrāsotsave ’sya bhuja-daṇḍa-gṛhīta-kaṇṭha-labdhāśiṣāṁ ya udagād vraja-vallabhīnām
verses.text: When Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa was dancing with the gopīs in the rāsa-līlā, the gopīs were embraced by the arms of the Lord. This transcendental favor was never bestowed upon the goddess of fortune or other consorts in the spiritual world. Indeed, never was such a thing even imagined by the most beautiful girls in the heavenly planets, whose bodily luster and aroma resemble the lotus flower. And what to speak of worldly women who are very beautiful according to material estimation?

verses.sanskrit: आसामहो चरणरेणुजुषामहं स्यांवृन्दावने किमपि गुल्मलतौषधीनाम् ।या दुस्त्यजं स्वजनमार्यपथं च हित्वाभेजुर्मुकुन्दपदवीं श्रुतिभिर्विमृग्याम् ॥ ६१ ॥
verses.transliteration: āsām aho caraṇa-reṇu-juṣām ahaṁ syāṁvṛndāvane kim api gulma-latauṣadhīnāmyā dustyajaṁ sva-janam ārya-pathaṁ ca hitvābhejur mukunda-padavīṁ śrutibhir vimṛgyām
verses.text: The gopīs of Vṛndāvana have given up the association of their husbands, sons and other family members, who are very difficult to give up, and they have forsaken the path of chastity to take shelter of the lotus feet of Mukunda, Kṛṣṇa, which one should search for by Vedic knowledge. Oh, let me be fortunate enough to be one of the bushes, creepers or herbs in Vṛndāvana, because the gopīs trample them and bless them with the dust of their lotus feet.

verses.sanskrit: या वै श्रियार्चितमजादिभिराप्तकामै-र्योगेश्वरैरपि यदात्मनि रासगोष्ठ्याम् ।कृष्णस्य तद् भगवत: चरणारविन्दंन्यस्तं स्तनेषु विजहु: परिरभ्य तापम् ॥ ६२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yā vai śriyārcitam ajādibhir āpta-kāmairyogeśvarair api yad ātmani rāsa-goṣṭhyāmkṛṣṇasya tad bhagavataḥ caraṇāravindaṁnyastaṁ staneṣu vijahuḥ parirabhya tāpam
verses.text: The goddess of fortune herself, along with Lord Brahmā and all the other demigods, who are masters of yogic perfection, can worship the lotus feet of Kṛṣṇa only within her mind. But during the rāsa dance Lord Kṛṣṇa placed His feet upon these gopīs’ breasts, and by embracing those feet the gopīs gave up all distress.

verses.sanskrit: वन्दे नन्दव्रजस्‍त्रीणां पादरेणुमभीक्ष्णश: ।यासां हरिकथोद्गीतं पुनाति भुवनत्रयम् ॥ ६३ ॥
verses.transliteration: vande nanda-vraja-strīṇāṁpāda-reṇum abhīkṣṇaśaḥyāsāṁ hari-kathodgītaṁpunāti bhuvana-trayam
verses.text: I repeatedly offer my respects to the dust from the feet of the women of Nanda Mahārāja’s cowherd village. When these gopīs loudly chant the glories of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the vibration purifies the three worlds.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचअथ गोपीरनुज्ञाप्य यशोदां नन्दमेव च ।गोपानामन्‍त्र्य दाशार्हो यास्यन्नारुरुहे रथम् ॥ ६४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaatha gopīr anujñāpyayaśodāṁ nandam eva cagopān āmantrya dāśārhoyāsyann āruruhe ratham
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Uddhava, the descendant of Daśārha, then took permission to leave from the gopīs and from mother Yaśodā and Nanda Mahārāja. He bade farewell to all the cowherd men and, about to depart, mounted his chariot.

verses.sanskrit: तं निर्गतं समासाद्य नानोपायनपाणय: ।नन्दादयोऽनुरागेण प्रावोचन्नश्रुलोचना: ॥ ६५ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ nirgataṁ samāsādyanānopāyana-pāṇayaḥnandādayo ’nurāgeṇaprāvocann aśru-locanāḥ
verses.text: As Uddhava was about to leave, Nanda and the others approached him bearing various items of worship. With tears in their eyes they addressed him as follows.

verses.sanskrit: मनसो वृत्तयो न: स्यु: कृष्णपादाम्बुजाश्रया: ।वाचोऽभिधायिनीर्नाम्नां कायस्तत्प्रह्वणादिषु ॥ ६६ ॥
verses.transliteration: manaso vṛttayo naḥ syuḥkṛṣṇa pādāmbujāśrayāḥvāco ’bhidhāyinīr nāmnāṁkāyas tat-prahvaṇādiṣu
verses.text: [Nanda and the other cowherds said:] May our mental functions always take shelter of Kṛṣṇa’s lotus feet, may our words always chant His names, and may our bodies always bow down to Him and serve Him.

verses.sanskrit: कर्मभिर्भ्राम्यमाणानां यत्र क्व‍ापीश्वरेच्छया ।मङ्गलाचरितैर्दानै रतिर्न: कृष्ण ईश्वरे ॥ ६७ ॥
verses.transliteration: karmabhir bhrāmyamāṇānāṁyatra kvāpīśvarecchayāmaṅgalācaritair dānairatir naḥ kṛṣṇa īśvare
verses.text: Wherever we are made to wander about this world by the Supreme Lord’s will, in accordance with the reactions to our fruitive work, may our good works and charity always grant Us love for Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: एवं सभाजितो गोपै: कृष्णभक्त्या नराधिप ।उद्धव: पुनरागच्छन्मथुरां कृष्णपालिताम् ॥ ६८ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ sabhājito gopaiḥkṛṣṇa-bhaktyā narādhipauddhavaḥ punar āgacchanmathurāṁ kṛṣṇa-pālitām
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] O ruler of men, thus honored by the cowherd men with expressions of devotion for Lord Kṛṣṇa, Uddhava went back to the city of Mathurā, which was under Kṛṣṇa’s protection.

verses.sanskrit: कृष्णाय प्रणिपत्याह भक्त्युद्रेकं व्रजौकसाम् ।वसुदेवाय रामाय राज्ञे चोपायनान्यदात् ॥ ६९ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛṣṇāya praṇipatyāhabhakty-udrekaṁ vrajaukasāmvasudevāya rāmāyarājñe copāyanāny adāt
verses.text: After falling down to pay his homage, Uddhava described to Lord Kṛṣṇa the immense devotion of the residents of Vraja. Uddhava also described it to Vasudeva, Lord Balarāma and King Ugrasena and presented to them the gifts of tribute he had brought with him.
